rationale: Portugal is geographically distant from most headline events; the most relevant development is the potential Iran peace deal and Hormuz reopening, which could lower energy prices affecting Portuguese households and the broader EU economy.

top_events.title: Iran war: US and Tehran signal peace deal; Hormuz reopening possible
top_events.summary: A ceasefire and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz could meaningfully lower oil and gas prices in Europe, reducing energy costs for Portuguese households and businesses still recovering from post-2022 inflation.

top_events.title: EU agrees launch of accession process for Ukraine and Moldova
top_events.summary: EU enlargement affects EU budget dynamics and political direction, with indirect relevance to Portugal as a net recipient of EU cohesion funds; long-term impact uncertain.

top_events.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX debuts on stock market
top_events.summary: SpaceX's stock market debut and Musk's wealth milestone reflect broader tech/financial market trends with marginal relevance to Portuguese investors and pension funds exposed to US equities.
